understanding.function: Nuclear fuel reprocessing system for extracting fissile material (uranium and plutonium) from spent nuclear fuel waste, capable of processing multiple fuel types (UOX, MOX, ERU/REU) with 1800 tHM/year capacity
understanding.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - spent fuel reprocessing and fissile material recovery
constraints: Product purity standards for downstream fuel fabrication
uncertainties: Specific technical processes and equipment details not defined
Exact safety and regulatory requirements beyond ALARA principle
Interface specifications with upstream waste reception and downstream fuel fabrication systems
Detailed operational parameters and control systems
Emergency response and degraded operation procedures

candidates.name: PUREX Process with Complexant-Based Stripping
candidates.description: Advanced PUREX process utilizing complexant-based stripping for enhanced uranium/plutonium separation in nuclear fuel reprocessing
candidates.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - spent fuel reprocessing
candidates.function_alignment: Direct alignment - provides uranium and plutonium extraction from spent nuclear fuel using proven industrial process
candidates.notes: Recent developments in 2005 focusing on improved separation efficiency

candidates.name: PUREX (Plutonium Uranium Redox Extraction) Process
candidates.description: Established aqueous reprocessing method using tributyl phosphate (TBP) in kerosene to separate uranium and plutonium from fission products through solvent extraction. Widely used at industrial scale including La Hague facility.
candidates.function_alignment: Direct - primary industrial method for uranium/plutonium extraction from spent fuel with proven multi-fuel capability
candidates.notes: Most mature technology for large-scale reprocessing, handles UOX/MOX/ERU fuel types, established safety protocols

candidates.name: COEX (Co-Extraction) Process
candidates.description: Advanced aqueous reprocessing technique that co-extracts uranium and plutonium together to reduce proliferation risks while maintaining separation efficiency. Developed as evolution of PUREX.
candidates.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - advanced aqueous reprocessing
candidates.function_alignment: High - addresses proliferation concerns while maintaining fissile material recovery capability for multiple fuel types
candidates.notes: Enhanced proliferation resistance, suitable for high-capacity operations, compatible with existing infrastructure

candidates.name: Pyroprocessing (Electrochemical Reprocessing)
candidates.description: High-temperature molten salt electrochemical process for separating actinides from fission products. Uses electrorefining in molten LiCl-KCl salt at 500°C to recover uranium and transuranics.
candidates.function_alignment: Moderate - alternative dry process with inherent proliferation resistance but lower throughput than aqueous methods
candidates.notes: Compact footprint, handles diverse fuel types, produces mixed actinide products, emerging technology

candidates.name: GANEX (Grouped Actinide Extraction) Process
candidates.description: Advanced solvent extraction process designed to co-extract all actinides (U, Np, Pu, Am, Cm) together, enabling partitioning and transmutation strategies while recovering fissile materials.
candidates.function_alignment: High - recovers fissile materials while enabling minor actinide management for waste reduction
candidates.notes: Supports closed fuel cycle, reduces long-term waste burden, compatible with Generation IV reactor concepts

candidates.name: UREX+ Process Suite
candidates.description: Modular aqueous reprocessing approach starting with uranium extraction (UREX) followed by optional additional separation steps. Designed for flexible implementation based on policy requirements.
candidates.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - modular aqueous reprocessing
candidates.function_alignment: High - flexible system allowing uranium recovery with optional plutonium separation based on policy needs
candidates.notes: Policy-flexible design, scalable capacity, proven chemistry basis, suitable for diverse fuel types

candidates.name: Advanced PUREX with Centrifugal Contactors
candidates.description: Enhanced PUREX implementation using centrifugal contactors for improved mass transfer, reduced solvent inventory, and enhanced safety through smaller equipment footprint and better process control.
candidates.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - advanced solvent extraction equipment
candidates.function_alignment: High - improves efficiency and safety of established fissile material recovery process
candidates.notes: Reduced criticality risk, improved process dynamics, suitable for high-throughput operations, proven technology

ranked.name: PUREX (Plutonium Uranium Redox Extraction) Process
ranked.description: Established aqueous reprocessing method using tributyl phosphate (TBP) in kerosene to separate uranium and plutonium from fission products through solvent extraction. Widely used at industrial scale including La Hague facility.
ranked.gap_analysis: Direct match for function - proven industrial technology for uranium/plutonium extraction at required capacity scale. Handles all specified fuel types (UOX, MOX, ERU).
ranked.notes: Most mature and widely deployed reprocessing technology, meets all capacity and multi-fuel requirements

ranked.name: COEX (Co-Extraction) Process
ranked.description: Advanced aqueous reprocessing technique that co-extracts uranium and plutonium together to reduce proliferation risks while maintaining separation efficiency. Developed as evolution of PUREX.
ranked.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - advanced aqueous reprocessing
ranked.gap_analysis: High alignment - addresses core function with enhanced proliferation resistance. Suitable for multi-fuel processing at industrial scale.
ranked.notes: Advanced evolution of PUREX with improved security features, compatible with existing infrastructure

ranked.name: Solvent Extraction Systems for Nuclear Power
ranked.description: Solvent extraction technologies specifically designed for nuclear power applications including fuel reprocessing
ranked.gap_analysis: High alignment - solvent extraction is the core separation technology for uranium/plutonium recovery in industrial reprocessing.
ranked.notes: Fundamental technology underlying all major reprocessing systems, well-established for nuclear applications

ranked.name: PUREX Process with Complexant-Based Stripping
ranked.description: Advanced PUREX process utilizing complexant-based stripping for enhanced uranium/plutonium separation in nuclear fuel reprocessing
ranked.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - spent fuel reprocessing
ranked.gap_analysis: Direct alignment - enhanced version of proven PUREX technology with improved separation efficiency for uranium and plutonium extraction.
ranked.notes: Evolutionary improvement to established PUREX process, maintains industrial scalability

ranked.name: Spent Fuel Dissolution and Reprocessing Systems
ranked.description: Comprehensive systems for dissolving spent nuclear fuel and subsequent reprocessing operations
ranked.gap_analysis: High alignment - covers essential first step (dissolution) and subsequent processing for fissile material recovery from multiple fuel types.
ranked.notes: Addresses complete dissolution-to-separation chain, fundamental to all aqueous reprocessing systems

ranked.name: Actinide Ion Extraction Chemistry Systems
ranked.description: Advanced extraction chemistry systems for processing actinide ions in nuclear fuel reprocessing
ranked.gap_analysis: High alignment - covers fundamental chemistry for uranium and plutonium (actinide) extraction, essential for all reprocessing systems.
ranked.notes: Foundational chemistry principles underlying industrial reprocessing, well-established science base

ranked.name: Process Intensification with Small Channel Flowsheets
ranked.description: Alternative flowsheet design using small channels for process intensification in spent nuclear fuel reprocessing
ranked.gap_analysis: Good alignment for function but uncertain scalability to 1800 tHM/year capacity. Innovative approach may not meet high-throughput requirements.
ranked.notes: Promising technology for efficiency but requires scale-up demonstration for industrial capacity

ranked.name: Organic Reductants for Pu and Np Ion Processing
ranked.description: Wet technology systems using organic reductants for plutonium and neptunium ion processing in spent fuel reprocessing
ranked.gap_analysis: Partial alignment - addresses plutonium processing chemistry but represents component technology rather than complete system solution.
ranked.notes: Specialized chemistry improvement for plutonium separation, requires integration with broader reprocessing system

ranked.name: Hydro-Process Separation Systems
ranked.description: Chemical separation systems using hydro-processes for recycling materials in nuclear fuel processing
ranked.gap_analysis: General alignment for material separation but lacks specific focus on fissile material extraction and nuclear-specific requirements.
ranked.notes: Broad separation technology applicable to nuclear processing but requires nuclear-specific adaptation

ranked.name: Radioactive Iodine and Krypton Control Systems
ranked.description: Control systems for managing radioactive iodine and krypton emissions in nuclear fuel reprocessing facilities
ranked.gap_analysis: Supporting function only - addresses radiological containment requirements but does not contribute to core fissile material extraction function.
ranked.notes: Essential supporting system for ALARA compliance but not primary reprocessing technology

ranked.name: Spent Radioactive Organic Solvent Treatment Systems
ranked.description: Treatment systems for managing spent radioactive organic solvents from nuclear fuel reprocessing operations
ranked.gap_analysis: Supporting function only - addresses waste management from reprocessing operations but does not contribute to fissile material extraction.
ranked.notes: Important auxiliary system for waste management but not core reprocessing functionality
